system_description.overview: Coframe is an AI-powered website optimization and personalization platform that uses advanced ML algorithms to generate, test, and refine website variations for conversion rate improvement. It offers A/B and multivariate testing, ML-based experimentation, continuous performance monitoring, and statistical analysis.

products.AI-Powered Website Optimization Platform: Automated generation and testing of website variations using advanced AI algorithms; ML-based testing and personalization engine; A/B split testing and multivariate testing; AI-driven content generation for headlines, body copy, CTAs, and images
products.Experiment Management Services: Full-service experiment design, deployment, and QA; integration with client analytics platforms
products.Analytics and Insights: Continuous performance monitoring, statistical analysis, competitive benchmarking

infrastructure.physical_presence: No physical offices; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (production servers at AWS maintain failover capabilities; hosted in high availability data centers with multiple availability zones)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: Hosted in AWS VPC; segmented VPCs and ACLs; customers' access appropriately segmented from other customer accounts
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via AWS; intrusion detection system utilized to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies
network_architecture.vpn: Remote access done through a bastion host with a VPN
network_architecture.tls: TLS (Transport Layer Security) with certificates from an established third-party CA
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server; internet access restricted
network_architecture.topology: User requests encrypted via TLS; AWS VPC with segmented subnets and ACLs; remote admin access via VPN/bastion host; IDS/IPS at network layer; firewalls on application gateway and production network

application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested in dev/test before promotion to production

databases: Not specifically named; Active Directory mentioned for user accounts
data_storage.backup_frequency: Not specifically stated; backup policy exists with defined retention periods

authentication_access_control.identity_provider: AWS (password policy controlled through AWS; Active Directory mentioned)
authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for user accounts with administrative access to production platform; MFA required for source code repository access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Controlled through AWS password policy; periodic forced changes; password expiry and complexity requirements; accounts locked after limited failed login attempts

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: AWS KMS (cloud provider key management service)

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Not named (source code management tool with MFA access)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured; every merge request to production requires additional approval
ci_cd_devops.iac: Baseline configurations retained as code; configuration management tool used for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO manages automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations receive automatic updates and scanned daily for compliance; critical/security OS patches applied as available

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named (monitoring tools used to detect anomalies)
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Not named (logging enabled at application and infrastructure level)
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts for high and critical risk events; incidents resolved via incident management process

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure / data center subservice organization

security_tools.antivirus: Endpoint security solution installed on all desktops and laptops; anti-virus installed on all production servers; daily automatic updates of virus definitions; continuous scanning
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

excluded_criteria: CC6.4 - Physical access to data centers: AWS is responsible for physical security
A1.2 - Environmental protections: Coframe does not manage its own office/environmental controls; managed by building management/AWS
Processing Integrity criteria - determined not relevant to the system
Privacy criteria - determined not relevant to the system
notable_controls: Quarterly user access reviews for all accounts including privileged users
Access revocation within one business day of termination
MFA enforced for production platform admin access and source code repo
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
IaC-based configuration management with rollback capability
BCDR plans tested at least annually across multiple loss scenarios
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) meets semiannually to review internal controls
Source code changes logged, time-stamped, attributed to author with MFA-protected access

infrastructure.rationale: AWS-hosted with VPC, multi-AZ failover, and IaC-based config management. No CDN mentioned, no multi-region, specific region not disclosed. Solid but not exceptional.

application_architecture.rationale: Separate environments, branch protection, code review enforced. No languages, frameworks, or architecture patterns disclosed. Very limited tech transparency.

data_layer.rationale: Multi-AZ, data classification policy, retention policy, and per-tenant segregation confirmed. However, no specific database technologies, caching, or backup frequency disclosed.

security_posture.rationale: Annual external pen test, monthly vuln scans, IDS/IPS, firewalls, MFA, endpoint protection, antivirus, quarterly access reviews. WAF not explicitly mentioned. No SIEM named.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC confirmed, branch protection and code review enforced, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews. However, CI/CD tool and source control platform not named.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy exists, annually reviewed and tested across multiple loss scenarios, multi-AZ failover. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover mentioned.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Only AWS named as a subservice organization. No other third-party vendors explicitly identified in the report. High concentration risk on AWS.

overall.rationale: Solid security governance and access control for an early-stage company. SOC 2 Type 2 with clean opinion is a positive signal. Major gaps are lack of technology transparency (no named CI/CD, source control, databases, or monitoring tools) and single cloud provider concentration.

red_flags: No specific database technologies disclosed - opaque data layer
No CI/CD pipeline or source control platform named (only 'source code management tool' referenced)
No monitoring/alerting tools named (APM, SIEM, log aggregation all unnamed)
Only one subservice organization (AWS) disclosed - high single-cloud concentration
No CDN mentioned despite operating a web optimization platform
yellow_flags: WAF not explicitly mentioned despite web-facing product
Application architecture pattern and tech stack completely undisclosed
green_flags: SOC 2 Type 2 with unqualified opinion - controls operated effectively over full period
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Access revoked within one business day of termination
MFA enforced for admin access to production and source code
IaC-based configuration management with rollback capability
BCDR tested annually across multiple scenarios
Multi-AZ production infrastructure with failover capabilities
Formal emergency change process with required approval and documentation
key_observations: Report covers Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy trust criteria - broad scope for a startup
Processing Integrity and Privacy were scoped as not relevant to the system, narrowing actual tested criteria to Security, Availability, and Confidentiality
Network diagram present in report (page 19) showing multiple AWS accounts/VPCs suggesting multi-account architecture
Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) is a governance body that meets semiannually - unusual governance formality for an early-stage company
The report explicitly excludes CC6.4 (physical access) and A1.2 (environmental) from scope due to full reliance on AWS - this is standard but worth noting
Source code access restricted to authorized users with MFA, suggesting GitHub or similar platform but not named
Company has no physical office and all infrastructure is cloud-native - reduces physical security risk but increases cloud concentration risk
